title: Product-supplied alias weakens transactional tenant validation
source: Authenticated merchant can create a product with its own storeId and arbitrary storeKeys; public caller selects that product id for another store.
impact: Cross-tenant product injection into another store order and inventory mutation/audit attribution across tenant boundaries.
control_gap: candidateStoreId from the product itself is added to expectedKeys, so a product accepted by a stale/forged alias validates its authoritative storeId.

title: Backorder arithmetic loses outstanding negative inventory
source: Existing stock object with allowBackorder=true and concurrent/sequential orders.
impact: Outstanding backorders are forgotten; cancellation can create positive sellable stock while other backorders remain.
control_gap: Every read clamps negative quantity to zero even though decrement deliberately writes negative values for backorders.

title: Cancellation can overwrite or reject a product after stock-format migration
source: Order inventory stores the historical stockFormat; merchant/admin migrates product format before cancellation.
impact: Legacy restore overwrites a current stock object with a number, or object restore targets a current numeric field and aborts cancellation.
control_gap: Historical format selects the current write operation without reconciling the live product format.

title: Order price and eligibility remain stale across the inventory transaction
source: Anonymous caller repeatedly submits an order while a merchant changes price, visibility, availability or deletion state.
impact: An order can be committed with a stale lower price or a product that became unavailable after pre-read.
control_gap: The transactional reread checks tenant and stock only; order payload and totals remain based on the pre-read.
